        Featured Story
        HUMAN RIGHTS
        What a school closure reveals about Modi government's crackdown on Kashmir's institutions
        The closure of one of India-administered Kashmir’s largest Islamic seminaries has left hundreds of students displaced, with families and critics viewing the move as part of a wider crackdown on Muslim religious and educational institutions.
        More
        SpaceX launches its biggest, most beefed-up Starship yet on a test flight
        SpaceX launches its 12th Starship on uncrewed test flight from Texas, in high-stakes trial run of major upgrades to its next-generation spacecraft as Elon Musk's rocket company nears record-breaking public listing.
        Canada PM Carney says Alberta 'essential' to country as separatist plan independence vote
        Mark Carney describes the oil-rich western province as "essential" part of Canada, a day after the province's announcement of referendum on whether to separate.
        Israel kills 10 people in Lebanon, including rescuers and a child in a 'double tap' strike
        Despite agreeing to cease fire multiple times, Israel has killed 3,111 Lebanese people since February, including 123 medics, over 210 children, and nearly 300 women.
        US tightens green card process, requires applicants to apply from abroad
        New guidance targets nonimmigrants, including international students, temporary workers and tourists, whom USCIS says are admitted to the US for a limited period and a defined purpose.
